note: All 7 case(s) below were published directly to TestRail outside this repo's /fetch-section -> /migrate-section pipeline and were never tracked locally until this backfill (2026-07-23). Case IDs and creation dates indicate they predate this migration project. Content is taken verbatim (HTML converted to plain text only) -- no v2 style-rewrite, Plan Gates extraction check, or Suite 6 source_case_ids cross-reference has been done yet; grill_status is honestly marked not-tracked-by-repo rather than assumed confirmed. live TestRail section is named "Consent Logs Card" (missing "Recent") vs the doc's "Recent Consent Logs Card".

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the Recent consent logs card shows the empty state when no consents are recorded
cases.preconditions: User is on the Dashboard page. No visitor consents have been recorded yet (consent logging not yet producing logs).

steps.content: Observe the Recent consent logs card on the Dashboard.
steps.expected: The card empty state should display the message "No consent log found!" with the supporting text "Visitor consents may not have been recorded yet. Make sure you've enabled consent logging".

steps.content: Locate the "consent logging" text within the empty-state message.
steps.expected: "consent logging" should be rendered as a clickable link.

steps.content: Locate the "View all logs" control on the card.
steps.expected: The "View all logs" button should be present at the bottom of the card.

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the consent logging link in the Recent consent logs empty state navigates to Advanced Settings
cases.preconditions: User is on the Dashboard page. The Recent consent logs card is in its empty state (no consents recorded).

steps.content: Click the "consent logging" link in the Recent consent logs card empty-state message.
steps.expected: The Advanced Settings page should be displayed.

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the Recent consent logs card records an Accepted consent
cases.preconditions: A cookie banner is published on the user's website. User can access both the live website and the Dashboard.

steps.content: On the user's website, open the page where the cookie banner is displayed.
steps.expected: The cookie banner should be displayed with an "Accept All" button.

steps.content: Click "Accept All" on the cookie banner.
steps.expected: The banner should close and the "Consent Preferences" revisit button should be displayed.

steps.content: Navigate to the Dashboard and locate the Recent consent logs card.
steps.expected: The newly recorded consent should appear as a row in the Recent consent logs list.

steps.content: Read the Consent Status of the new log row.
steps.expected: The Consent Status should be "Accepted".

steps.content: Confirm the row shows a Consent ID and a Date/Time value.
steps.expected: The row should display a Consent ID and a Date/Time (UTC ± 00:00) value.

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the Recent consent logs card records a Rejected consent
cases.preconditions: A cookie banner is published on the user's website. User can access both the live website and the Dashboard.

steps.content: On the user's website, open the page where the cookie banner is displayed.
steps.expected: The cookie banner should be displayed with a "Reject All" button.

steps.content: Click "Reject All" on the cookie banner.
steps.expected: The banner should close and the "Consent Preferences" revisit button should be displayed.

steps.content: Navigate to the Dashboard and locate the Recent consent logs card.
steps.expected: The newly recorded consent should appear as a row in the Recent consent logs list.

steps.content: Read the Consent Status of the new log row.
steps.expected: The Consent Status should be "Rejected".

steps.content: Confirm the row shows a Consent ID and a Date/Time value.
steps.expected: The row should display a Consent ID and a Date/Time (UTC ± 00:00) value.

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the Recent consent logs card records a Partially-Accepted consent
cases.preconditions: A cookie banner with at least one non-Necessary category is published on the user's website. User can access both the live website and the Dashboard.

steps.content: On the user's website, open the cookie banner and click "Customize".
steps.expected: The second layer of the cookie banner with category toggles should be displayed.

steps.content: Enable the toggle for one category other than "Necessary".
steps.expected: The selected category toggle should be enabled.

steps.content: Click "Save My Preferences".
steps.expected: The banner should collapse and the revisit consent button should be displayed.

steps.content: Navigate to the Dashboard and locate the Recent consent logs card.
steps.expected: The newly recorded consent should appear as a row in the Recent consent logs list.

steps.content: Read the Consent Status of the new log row.
steps.expected: The Consent Status should be "Partially-Accepted".

steps.content: Confirm the row shows a Consent ID and a Date/Time value.
steps.expected: The row should display a Consent ID and a Date/Time (UTC ± 00:00) value.

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the Consent ID tooltip Learn more link opens the consent recording documentation
cases.preconditions: User is on the Dashboard page. At least one consent log exists so the Recent consent logs card displays the consent table.

steps.content: Hover over the information tooltip next to the "Consent ID" column header in the Recent consent logs card.
steps.expected: The Consent ID help tooltip should be displayed and should contain a "Learn more" link.

steps.content: Click the "Learn more" link in the tooltip.
steps.expected: The "How to Record User Consent for Cookies" documentation page should be displayed.

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the View all logs button opens the Consent Log page
cases.preconditions: User is on the Dashboard page with the Recent consent logs card displayed.

steps.content: Click the "View all logs" button at the bottom of the Recent consent logs card.
